content: Recall classification criteria: Class I requires reasonable probability that use/exposure will cause serious adverse health consequences or death (e.g., contamination with toxic substances, wrong strength of potent drug, absence of active ingredient in life-sustaining drug). Class II applies when use/exposure may cause temporary or medically reversible adverse health consequences, or where probability of serious effects is remote (e.g., minor labeling errors, particulate matter below action levels). Class III covers situations where use/exposure is not likely to cause adverse health consequences (e.g., minor defects in packaging, off-specification appearance without safety impact). Classification determines recall strategy depth and public notification requirements.
